2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents

4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art

- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents
  • Evidence: the Main Page visit's links_sample includes the Portal:Contents URL (see Main Page visit result). See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page -> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents

Missing explicit evidence in the provided results:
- The merged visit results do not include a link sample explicitly showing that https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Arts, so that adjacency cannot be independently verified from the supplied link samples.
- The results also do not include a link sample explicitly showing that https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Arts links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art, so that adjacency cannot be independently verified from the supplied link samples.

Sources (visited pages from merged results):
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Arts
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art
output.action_summary: I followed the visited pages in the merged results to produce a 4-page chain: Main Page → Portal:Contents → Portal:Arts → Art. The Main Page → Portal:Contents adjacency is explicitly supported by the Main Page visit links_sample (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents). The merged results do not include explicit link-sample evidence for Portal:Contents → Portal:Arts or Portal:Arts → Art, so those adjacencies cannot be independently verified from the provided data.

result.title: Think: Assemble chain and produce one verified adjacency pair (Portal:Arts -> Art)

details.intent: After the visits run, assemble the ordered chain of URLs from the visit outputs and produce one verified adjacency pair (show the exact inline snippet from Portal:Arts that links to Art).
plan_and_verification_steps: Receive _links_inline from Visit Portal:Arts result. Identify the entry whose URL equals the Art page URL.
Produce the chain array in order: [Main Page URL, Portal:Contents URL, Portal:Arts URL, Art URL].
For adjacency verification, output the exact Portal:Arts _links_inline string that contains '[link: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art]'; this serves as proof that Portal:Arts links to Art.
If Portal:Arts did not contain the Art link, fall back to selecting another adjacency pair from the last two pages that do contain a direct link, and report that instead (with exact snippet).
expected_output_fields: notes (string) — any fallback or verification issues
details.why: This reflective node compiles results and performs the required verification step (show one adjacency pair with exact page evidence). It's run after the visits so it can use concrete extracted link data.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

details.intent: Fetch the Main Page and extract its outgoing wiki links to start the chain.
target_facts: List of inline links on the Main Page in the form 'Link text [link: <URL>]' (field: _links_inline)
Find candidate portal/article links suitable for continuing the chain (e.g., Portal:Contents, Portal:Current_events, Portal:Community)
details.extraction_plan: Download page HTML/text, extract all internal https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/* links and return them in _links_inline. Produce a short list (3–6) of high-confidence next-step URLs with link text and exact URL strings.
details.why: This is the mandated starting URL. We must read the Main Page to get exact link URLs (per tool rules: subsequent visits must use URLs discovered on prior pages). This reduces uncertainty by producing concrete URLs to visit next.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. 82df578c-bdbf-4d05-8eaf-592db9ede9fd.title: Visit Portal:Contents (follow a Main Page link)

details.intent: Fetch Portal:Contents to extract its outgoing wiki links and choose the next page in the chain.
target_facts: Confirm that Portal:Contents was present on the Main Page's link list (verification will be cross-checked against Main Page _links_inline).
Extract Portal:Contents outgoing links (field: _links_inline) and produce candidate next URLs (likely Portal pages such as Portal:Arts, Portal:Culture, Portal:Geography).
details.extraction_plan: Download Portal:Contents page, extract internal wiki links; produce a ranked list of candidate next pages and capture exact URL strings in the '[link: ...]' format.
output_fields: verification_mainpage_inclusion (boolean) — whether this URL appeared in Main Page's _links_inline
details.why: Portal:Contents is a natural next step from the Main Page 'Contents' box. Visiting it yields explicit portal links we can use for subsequent steps and allows verification that the link chain obeys the rule 'each next URL must come from links found on the previous page.'
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

details.why: Portal:Arts typically links to the general 'Art' article and others. Capturing the exact inline link that points to the next page gives the verifiable adjacency evidence required by the mandate.

2357963c-b433-474c-b196-1446a99afb63.title: Think: Assemble chain and produce one verified adjacency pair (Portal:Arts -> Art)

details.intent: After the visits run, assemble the ordered chain of URLs from the visit outputs and produce one verified adjacency pair (show the exact inline snippet from Portal:Arts that links to Art).
plan_and_verification_steps: Receive _links_inline from Visit Portal:Arts result. Identify the entry whose URL equals the Art page URL.
Produce the chain array in order: [Main Page URL, Portal:Contents URL, Portal:Arts URL, Art URL].
For adjacency verification, output the exact Portal:Arts _links_inline string that contains '[link: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art]'; this serves as proof that Portal:Arts links to Art.
If Portal:Arts did not contain the Art link, fall back to selecting another adjacency pair from the last two pages that do contain a direct link, and report that instead (with exact snippet).
expected_output_fields: notes (string) — any fallback or verification issues
details.why: This reflective node compiles results and performs the required verification step (show one adjacency pair with exact page evidence). It's run after the visits so it can use concrete extracted link data.

action_result.title: Think: Assemble chain and produce one verified adjacency pair (Portal:Arts -> Art)

details.intent: After the visits run, assemble the ordered chain of URLs from the visit outputs and produce one verified adjacency pair (show the exact inline snippet from Portal:Arts that links to Art).
plan_and_verification_steps: Receive _links_inline from Visit Portal:Arts result. Identify the entry whose URL equals the Art page URL.
Produce the chain array in order: [Main Page URL, Portal:Contents URL, Portal:Arts URL, Art URL].
For adjacency verification, output the exact Portal:Arts _links_inline string that contains '[link: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Art]'; this serves as proof that Portal:Arts links to Art.
If Portal:Arts did not contain the Art link, fall back to selecting another adjacency pair from the last two pages that do contain a direct link, and report that instead (with exact snippet).
